course_descriptor: LazyLearn track, Michael Sandel moral and political philosophy lecture series
deliverable_target: a faithful, source-conscious chapter for Justice, plus a per-course compiled PDF book.
visual_target: selected lecture and broadcast frames are only supporting evidence for names, prompts, on-screen text, and discussion structure; do not let them override the spoken content.
mathematical_standard: preserve the conceptual distinctions, examples, thought experiments, and argumentative structure from the lectures, but avoid generic filler or invented textbook exposition.
style_target: notes should read like polished companion notes for a serious public philosophy course, with explicit structure, careful distinctions between arguments and objections, and clear treatment of examples and principles.
credit_target: keep explicit credit to Michael Sandel and curation by LazyingArt LLC; reserve the website URL for the generated front-matter credit, not the prose body.
front_matter_single: Original lecture by Michael Sandel. Lecture notes organized by \href{https://lazying.art}{LazyingArt LLC}.
front_matter_plural: Original lectures by Michael Sandel. Lecture notes organized by \href{https://lazying.art}{LazyingArt LLC}.
reference_missing_text: No directly matching reference PDF is available for this course.
reference_no_match_text: Matching course reference PDFs were found, but no strong topical excerpt was automatically matched for this lecture.
reference_budget_text: Matching course reference PDFs were found, but no excerpt fit within the current prompt budget.
